MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MONACA SHANKS' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Before the court is an unopposed Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel Sutton A. Shapiro and Wilshire Law Firm, counsel of record for Plaintiff Monaca Laureen Shanks. OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Counsel neither timely filed nor timely served their moving papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing is within State of California adds five calendar days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Moving Counsel filed and served their motion on November 6, 2025, which is not 16 court days plus two court days before 12/1/2025, the hearing date noticed.

Counsel is reminded conclusory statements are insufficient. The facts supporting the conclusion need to be set out, if possible, in the declaration. If counsel re-files and seeks an in camera hearing, counsel's in-person attendance will be required at the hearing.

Prior to the hearing, Moving Counsel shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

No proposed order received. Moving counsel failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-04-2025 Continued To Dec-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Hemant Patel's unopposed Motion To Compel Plaintiff Monica Laureen Shanks' Independent Medical Examination is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Good cause appearing and pursuant to the California Discovery Act, Plaintiff is ordered to submit to an independent medical examination consistent with the specifics set out in Defendant Patel's October 31, 2024, Demand. (See Culley Decl., ex. A; Code of Civil Procedure section 2032,310.) Plaintiff shall submit to the IME forthwith but no later than August 1, 2025. Parties must meet and confer regarding the specific date of the IME.

Defendant's request for sanctions is denied on there independent grounds. Defendant failed to identify a specific statutory basis for sanctions under these circumstances. Defendant did not establish an express stipulation for or a court order compelling an IME, so it is not clear it was appropriate prior to this order. Finally, Defendant did not provide a requested amount or any support for the request.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
